title: branch — outbound webhooks
description: Tenant admins can subscribe to branch-lifecycle events via the webhook module (shared/webhooks, Wave 4). This blueprint declares the event types this module publishes. Payload shapes mirror the corresponding entries in `events.json` — the webhook module wraps them in the standard envelope + HMAC-signs with the tenant's webhook secret.

event_types.description: New Branch provisioned. Payload mirrors BranchCreated event.
Branch fields (not address / coordinates / hours / capacity) updated. Payload mirrors BranchUpdated event with `changed_fields`.
Umbrella event for any status transition. Payload mirrors BranchStatusChanged.
Branch transitioned active → paused. Payload mirrors BranchPaused.
Branch transitioned to closed. Payload includes `successor_branch_id` when set — external systems syncing the tenant's roster use this to redirect operations.
Branch transitioned closed → active. Rare — most external systems don't listen for this.
The default branch for a (tenant, organization) tuple was reassigned. Payload mirrors BranchDefaultChanged.
Branch.address changed. Payload includes the full new address + `changed_address_fields`.
Branch.coordinates changed. Payload includes the new coordinates AND the source (manual / geocoded / imported). External CRM systems syncing venue pin locations use this.
Branch.opening_hours changed. Payload includes the full new opening_hours blob — external scheduling systems consume this directly.
Branch.capacity changed. Payload includes from/to values (or null for cleared).

notes: Every payload is HMAC-SHA256 signed with the tenant's webhook secret per the shared webhook contract.

The `branch.deleted` event is NOT published to webhooks — public API never hard-deletes; the platform-support CLI path deliberately bypasses webhooks because the tenant contract is void at that point.

External systems syncing venue rosters should subscribe to `branch.created` + `branch.status_changed` + `branch.address_updated` + `branch.coordinates_updated` at minimum. `branch.updated` is optional — the changed_fields tell the subscriber whether to re-fetch.
